channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Uniper Secures Customer for Polish Solar Portfolio
item.description: Respect Energy signed a six-year agreement to buy 100 percent of the power generated, as well as guarantees of origin, from four Uniper photovoltaic projects with a combined capacity of 219 megawatts peak in Poland.

item.title: Ukraine Hits Russia's Biggest Refinery
item.description: Ukraine struck three Russian refineries, including the country's biggest.

item.title: BP, JERA Raise Stake in 2 Belgian Offshore Wind Farms
item.description: JERA Nex bp, the 50:50 offshore wind joint venture of Japan's JERA and Britain's BP, completed the purchase of Sumitomo's stakes in two Belgian offshore wind facilities with a combined capacity of 384 megawatts.

item.title: Which Country is the World's Biggest Energy Supplier?
item.description: Rigzone examines the Energy Institute's latest statistical review of world energy report, which was released recently.

item.title: Elixir Energy’s Lorelle-3H begins transition to gas production
item.description: Elixir Energy’s Lorelle-3H well has begun transitioning from fluid to gas production in ATP2056, within Queensland’s Taroom Trough, after recording sustained gas breakthrough five days into clean-up operations.

item.title: Carbon Markets Just Had Their Most Important Moment in Years
item.description: For much of the past decade, carbon credits have suffered from a credibility problem. Critics called them greenwashing, environmental groups questioned their effectiveness, and a series of poorly designed projects…

item.title: UK's $667 Billion Net-Zero Bill Could Make Britain Uncompetitive
item.description: The UK's cost to meet its ambitious net-zero and clean power goals is a massive $667 billion, or £500 billion, which would be heavily front-loaded capital expenditure that will hurt businesses and bill payers, according…

item.title: Who Are the Most Prolific Private Oil, Gas Producers in USA?
item.description: Enverus announced that it has published its annual list of 'the 100 most prolific private oil and gas producers in the United States'.

item.title: Pakistan Bypasses Afghanistan With New Corridors Through Iran and China
item.description: Pakistan has approved and operationalized new land routes to connect Central Asian markets to Pakistani ports and beyond, utilizing strategic corridors through Iran and China to bypass Afghanistan entirely. This shift…

item.title: Oil exports from the Middle East rising, but risks remain – IEA
item.description: The International Energy Agency says oil prices have eased as exports through the Strait of Hormuz begin recovering.

item.title: India's Power Demand Set to Grow 6% a Year Through 2030
item.description: Power demand in India is expected to rise by about 6% every year over the next four-five years, with growth supported by renewable energy capacity additions, brokerage Centrum Institutional Research said in a report on…

item.title: OPEC+ Decides to Boost Production Further in August
item.description: 'Seven OPEC+ countries ... met virtually on 5 July 2026, to review global market conditions and outlook,” a statement posted on OPEC's site highlighted.

item.title: Oil drops more than 1% after Opec+ raises August output targets
item.description: Oil prices declined by more than 1% on 6 July, Monday, following the move by the Organisation of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) and its allies, known as OPEC+, to raise production targets from August.

item.title: Washington's Plan to Neutralize Iran’s Hormuz Leverage
item.description: For decades, Iran has relied on the Strait of Hormuz as its most effective geopolitical weapon -- a crucial energy chokepoint through which up to a third of the world’s oil is transported and about a fifth of its…

item.title: Shell, Local Banks Offer $3B Credit Scheme to Nigerian Contractors
item.description: Shell and nine Nigerian banks launched a $3-billion Contract Finance Facility for local companies executing projects for the British energy giant in the West African country.

item.title: Fear of an Oil Glut May Be Overblown, Top Consultancy Says
item.description: Up to 75% of the previous oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz are expected to return to the market by the end of the year, but significantly lower oil prices aren’t guaranteed for 2027 as the ongoing U.S.-Iran…

item.title: Glenfarne, BGN sign HoA for LNG supply from Texas LNG
item.description: Glenfarne Global Commodities, the liquefied natural gas (LNG) marketing and trading arm of Glenfarne Group, has signed a heads of agreement (HoA) with BGN for the long-term supply of one million tonnes per annum (mtpa)…

item.title: Trump to Meet Zelenskyy at NATO Summit as Ukraine War Diplomacy Resumes
item.description: US President Donald Trump will meet Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy on the sidelines of this week's NATO summit in Ankara, the White House announced on July 5, as Washington seeks to revive stalled diplomacy over…

item.title: ADNOC Consolidates LNG Sales Activities
item.description: ADNOC's new LNG marketing and trading platform targets 47 million metric tons per annum in marketed volumes by 2035.

item.title: Hormuz Tanker Traffic Slowly Recovers Despite Persistent Risks
item.description: Tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has picked up pace in recent weeks, but oil flows are anything but smooth. At least six tankers carrying oil or LNG moved to exit the Persian Gulf through Hormuz on a route…
